feuds:
Melissa Gorga: Did not invite Melissa's mother Donna to her wedding; accused of only using family when convenient; clashes over Louie's motivations for joining the show
Melissa Gorga: Accused of lying about Louie wanting to date Alexia (from Miami) before meeting Teresa
key_moments:
Defends not inviting Melissa's mom Donna to the wedding by citing 'history' and not wanting to invite her without the rest of the family
Claims she has photos proving she included Melissa socially (dinners with Shane, the neighbor), countering Melissa's claim of exclusion
Drops bombshell alleging Louie wanted to be on the show and was interested in Alexia from Miami before meeting Teresa, prompting Melissa to call her a liar
Says 'I can't wait' when Melissa hints she's leaving, escalating the rift
talking_points:
Melissa's mother was not intentionally excluded — there's a history there, and inviting one person without the full family felt wrong
You have receipts (photos) showing you included Melissa socially during the hard times — don't let the 'you only call on us when you need us' narrative go unchallenged
Stand firm on the Louie/Alexia story — you were told this information and you're not backing down
Address the 'coexistence' question directly: what does your relationship with Melissa look like going forward?
Acknowledge that Joe brought Louie onto the show, but make clear Louie was already interested in the spotlight
confessional_draft: I have been nothing but transparent about why Donna wasn't at my wedding, and yet here we are — again — being painted as the villain of my own love story. Louie wanted to be on television before he even met me, and I'm sorry, but the truth doesn't stop being the truth just because it's inconvenient for Melissa. After everything I've been through, I refuse to let anyone rewrite my history.

feuds:
Teresa Giudice: Furious that her mother Donna was not invited to Teresa's wedding; accuses Teresa of only leaning on family when it suits her
Teresa Giudice: Defends Louie against Teresa's claim that he pursued Alexia and wanted to be on TV; calls Teresa a liar
key_moments:
Points out that not inviting Melissa's mom was 'no oversight,' framing it as intentional disrespect
Invokes Teresa using Joe's side of the family when she needed support post-split ('when you need us, you're good; when you don't, you're like I'm good, thanks')
Fires back at the Shane/dinner story, saying Teresa 'invited herself' and was never actually included by them
Calls Teresa a liar on the Louie/Alexia claim: 'You've been doing this for years, it's getting old'
Implies she may be done — hinting at leaving — prompting Teresa to say 'I can't wait'
talking_points:
Not inviting your mother to the wedding was a deliberate slight — own that talking point and don't let Teresa's 'family history' excuse slide
The 'you only need us when it's convenient' argument is your strongest emotional beat — stay on it
The Shane/dinner story is a distraction — redirect to the bigger picture of the pattern of exclusion
Address the Louie allegations head-on: you know your husband, and Teresa's claims are baseless
Be clear about where you stand on the future of your relationship with Teresa — vague hints about leaving only let her control the narrative
confessional_draft: My mother deserved to be at that wedding — period. Teresa calls on us when she needs us and drops us the second she doesn't, and I am done pretending that pattern doesn't exist. Calling my husband a fame-chaser is the lowest she's ever gone, and honestly? It might just be the last straw.

key_moments: Briefly addressed by Andy when he asks whether Teresa leaving would mean Melissa is out of her life — Angie acknowledges they still have 'a couple hours' left at the reunion, serving as a minor mediating voice
talking_points:
Position yourself as the calm observer who can speak to both sides of the Teresa-Melissa feud
Be ready to weigh in on the wedding invite situation with your own perspective on Italian family dynamics
Don't get lost in the shuffle — find your moment to assert your own storyline amid the Teresa-Melissa dominance
confessional_draft: Everyone is so focused on Teresa and Melissa that they forget there are other women at this table with real things to say. I've watched this back-and-forth for months, and tonight, sitting here live, it's even more exhausting than it looked on TV.
